mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which leads to higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is always on and direction is positive.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal generates negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market weakness, which reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is triggered only when monthly UNRATE change exceeds 0.2 percentage points.
failure_condition: In instances triggered by UNRATE spike, the average 20-day forward return is non-negative, or the sign of returns is positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are above 2000 thousand units and the federal funds rate has increased by more than 0.25% over three months, the combination signals an overheating housing market that will likely correct, dragging down broader equity markets over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold (HOUST > 2000k and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%), future 60-day S&P 500 return is positive more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime defined by CPI YoY growth above 3%, strong GDP growth signals an overheating economy that prompts tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is only active when CPI YoY exceeds 3%.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY > 3% and GDP is above trend, the average 20-day forward return is positive, or the negative signal fails in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls over the past month indicates robust job growth, which boosts consumer income and confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This signal is always on.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following positive monthly payrolls change is negative on average over a 2-year period, or the Rank IC of payrolls change is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3%, it indicates the Fed is tightening monetary policy sufficiently to slow economic activity, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is positive or the signal produces a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising (3-month growth above 1%) but nonfarm payrolls are falling (monthly decline), it signals a productivity-driven expansion without job creation, which is unsustainable and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the divergence condition holds (INDPRO up >1% and PAYEMS down), the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a very tight labor market defined by the unemployment rate below 4%, further declines in unemployment signal excessive tightness that will lead to wage inflation and Fed tightening, ultimately dragging equity prices down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE < 4%, the average 20-day forward return is positive, or the negative signal has a hit rate below 50%.
